item.description: Many have tried but few succeed

item.description: Chaos and instability are a feature, not a bug, of the US president’s approach to foreign policy

item.title: The Iran war presents a ‘guns and butter’ nightmare for investors
item.description: Conflict will intensify the mounting global debt and inflation risks

item.title: Why oil at $200 a barrel is no longer unthinkable
item.description: If supply disruptions persist, holders of stockpiled reserves can’t be relied on to fully cushion the impact

item.title: Will your garden endure as your legacy?
item.description: As life replaces death in springtime, gardeners can’t help but ponder some existential questions

item.title: The AI PR problem
item.description: Sector companies have been slow to address the concerns of ordinary people over the technology

item.title: Lessons from Anthropic’s battle with the Pentagon
item.description: Guardrails around the state’s use of AI warrant deeper and democratic deliberation

item.title: Bankers’ bumper year for IPOs isn’t quite going to plan
item.description: With volatility high, few companies hoping for a credible first-day performance want to brave choppy waters.

item.title: Merz should seize the opportunity to push back against Trump
item.description: Unlike his predecessors, Germany’s chancellor does not define himself by constraints

item.title: Iran could collapse from the periphery, not the centre
item.description: As the state weakens, minority groups in the borderlands could chip away at the country’s territorial integrity

item.title: In defence of hand-wringers and pearl-clutchers
item.description: To surrender the fight for international law is to accept worse outcomes and more civilian deaths

item.title: The economic question we forget to ask about AI
item.description: Forget the singularity and robot takeovers; what matters is who captures the value added

item.title: Iran attacks show the perils of following America’s economic lead
item.description: A growth plan of renewable energy and diversified trade is far better than guzzling fossil fuels and aligning with the US

item.description: How the hours we spend at our job have changed over the years

item.title: How the UK mortgage market became so unstable
item.description: The market has not been this jittery in decades — and that was before the Middle East conflict

item.description: It will take time for a durable equity base to be formed after momentum has been broken

item.title: Can Nvidia’s margins last?
item.description: Chipmaker’s symbiotic relationship with TSMC has been lucrative but it is also a key vulnerability

item.title: Three reasons to be hopeful for UK homebuilders
item.description: Despite Vistry’s gloomy outgoing chief, easier planning rules, better affordability and possibly even state help could boost sector
